description: Learn how to quickly create and deploy an Azure confidential virtual machine (confidential VM) using an ARM template.
summary: You can use an Azure Resource Manager template (ARM template) to create an Azureconfidential VMquickly. Confidential VMs run on both AMD processors backed by AMD SEV-SNP and Intel processors backed by Intel TDX to achieve VM memory encryption and isolation. For more information, seeConfidential VM Overview. This tutorial covers deployment of a confidential VM with a custom configuration.
classification_reason: ARM template quickstart for confidential VMs; focuses on basic deployment rather than detailed configuration options, limits, or decision matrices.

title: Secret and Key Management in Azure Confidential Computing
display_title: Manage secrets and keys in Azure confidential workloads
description: This article helps you to understand how confidential computing handles secrets and keys.
summary: Confidential computing provides advanced capabilities for protecting secrets and keys while they're in use to enhance the security posture of an application. Confidential computing-enabled services use keys managed by thehardware root of trustto informattestationservices and encrypt and decrypt data inside the Trusted Execution Environment (TEE). Keys are an important part of protection for confidential virtual machines (CVMs) and many other services built on CVMs likeconfidential node pools on
classification_reason: Describes how confidential computing-enabled services handle keys and secrets using hardware root of trust and attestation; this is product-specific security behavior beyond generic key management concepts.

summary: Secure Key Release (SKR) flow with Azure Key Vault (AKV) with confidential container offerings can implement in couple of ways. Confidential containers run a guest enlightened exposing AMD SEV-SNP device through a Linux Kernel that uses an in guest firmware with necessary Hyper-V related patches that we refer as Direct Linux Boot (DLB). This platform doesn't use vTPM and HCL based that Confidential VMs with AMD SEV-SNP support. This concept document assumes you plan to run the containers inAzure
classification_reason: Covers SKR integration for confidential containers on ACI, including platform-specific behavior (DLB, no vTPM) and how to wire AKV, MAA, and container runtime together.

summary: Secure Key Release (SKR) can only release exportable marked keys based on the Microsoft Azure Attestation (MAA) generated claims. There's a tight integration on the SKR policy definition to MAA claims. MAA claims by trusted execution environment (TEE) can be foundhere. Follow the policygrammarfor more examples on how you can customize the SKR policies.
classification_reason: Provides concrete SKR policy examples tied to MAA claims and TEE types, including policy grammar usage and claim mappings that are highly product-specific security configuration knowledge.

title: Trusted Computing Base (TCB) in Azure Confidential Computing
description: This article helps you to understand what the TCB is and what it includes.
summary: Trusted computing base(TCB) refers to all of a system's hardware, firmware, and software components that provide a secure environment. The components inside the TCB are considered critical. If one component inside the TCB is compromised, the entire system's security might be jeopardized. A lower TCB means higher security. classification_reason: Conceptual description of Trusted Computing Base; no concrete Azure configuration settings, limits, or decision matrices.

title: Azure Confidential VM options
display_title: Choose Azure confidential VM options on AMD or Intel
description: Azure Confidential Computing offers multiple options for confidential virtual machines on AMD and Intel processors.
summary: Azure offers a choice of Trusted Execution Environment (TEE) options from both AMD and Intel. These TEEs allow you to create Confidential VM environments with excellent price-to-performance ratios, all without requiring any code changes. For AMD-based Confidential VMs, the technology used isAMD SEV-SNP, which was introduced with 3rd Gen AMD EPYC™ processors. classification_reason: Compares AMD SEV-SNP and Intel TDX-based confidential VM options and their characteristics, helping select the appropriate TEE/VM family.

display_title: Use virtual TPMs in Azure confidential virtual machines
description: Learn about how we use virtual Trusted Platform Modules in our confidential VMs.

These functions include secret storage for cryptographic keys, storage for measurements of the boot process and an external hardware root-of-trust. Azure confidential VMs each have their own dedicated virtual TPM (vTPM). The vTPM is a virtualized version of a hardware TPM, and complies with theTPM2.0 spec.
